title: UNSW-NB15 to IDS Output Schema Mapping
description: Explicit column-level mapping from UNSW-NB15 dataset (43 columns) to target IDS output schema (21 columns), including identity mappings, aggregations, generated/inferred columns, and dropped features.

unsw_schema.description: Original UNSW-NB15 dataset columns (45 total: 1 ID + 42 features + 2 labels)

columns.name: service
columns.description: Service type (http, ssh, dns, ftp, etc.)

columns.name: ct_srv_src
columns.description: Count connections to service from source
columns.handling: DROPPED (temporal context, hard to preserve in fixed window)

columns.name: ct_dst_ltm
columns.description: Count connections to destination in last time interval

columns.name: ct_src_dport_ltm
columns.description: Count source IPs to same dest port in last time interval
columns.handling: IDENTITY → ct_src_dport_ltm (contextual, preserved for anomaly detection)

columns.name: ct_dst_sport_ltm
columns.description: Count with same dest sport in last time interval

columns.name: ct_dst_src_ltm
columns.description: Count unique dest IPs from same source in last time interval
columns.handling: IDENTITY → ct_dst_src_ltm (contextual, preserved for anomaly detection)

columns.name: ct_src_ltm
columns.description: Count source IPs in last time interval

columns.name: attack_cat
columns.description: Attack category (Normal, Exploits, Worms, Backdoor, Shellcode)

columns.name: label
columns.handling: IDENTITY → label (converted to Benign/Malicious/False Alarm in Step 6)

ids_schema.description: Target IDS output schema (21 columns required for NoDOZE alignment)

columns.name: timestamp
columns.source: GENERATED (synthetic, assigned in Step 6 based on temporal phase architecture)

columns.name: src_host
columns.description: Source hostname (must belong to defined topology)
columns.constraints: User0-4 | Enterprise0-2 | Defender | OpHost0-2 | OpServer0 | external_XXXX
columns.source: GENERATED (deterministic IP→hostname mapping using MD5(IP+scenario_id) hash in Pre-Step)

columns.name: dst_host
columns.description: Destination hostname (must belong to defined topology)
columns.constraints: User0-4 | Enterprise0-2 | Defender | OpHost0-2 | OpServer0 | external_XXXX
columns.source: GENERATED (deterministic IP→hostname mapping using MD5(IP+scenario_id) hash in Pre-Step)

columns.name: sport
columns.source: GENERATED (random ephemeral port; not in UNSW; assigned in Pre-Step)

columns.name: dport
columns.constraints: Scenario-specific; must align with service (80=http, 22=ssh, 53=dns, 21=ftp, 445=smb, etc.)
columns.source: GENERATED (inferred from UNSW service field using port-service lookup table in Pre-Step)

columns.name: service
columns.constraints: http | ftp | ssh | dns | smtp | smb | snmp | rdp | -

columns.name: state
columns.constraints: FIN | CON | INT | REQ | RST | URP | FOU | FSRA

columns.name: ct_src_dport_ltm
columns.description: Count source IPs to same dest port in last time interval (contextual for port scanning detection)

columns.name: ct_dst_src_ltm
columns.description: Count unique dest IPs from same source in last time interval (contextual for lateral movement detection)

columns.name: label
columns.source: GENERATED (derived from label distribution and synthesis strategy in Steps 3-6; not a direct UNSW mapping)

IDENTITY.description: Direct pass-through: UNSW column value used unchanged in IDS output
IDENTITY.rationale: These features are directional-agnostic or already aggregated; no transformation needed

AGGREGATION.description: Combine directional UNSW columns into single aggregated feature

transformations.rationale: IDS perspective is protocol-neutral; total bytes transferred matters more than directionality
Total packet count is more relevant for IDS detection than directional split

AGGREGATION.correlation_preservation: Ensure Corr(bytes, packets) and Corr(bytes, duration) maintained within ±5% of original UNSW statistics

GENERATION.description: Synthetic creation: column values generated based on rules, not derived from UNSW data

columns.generation_rule: Assigned in Step 6 using phase-based temporal architecture; strictly increasing in range [0, 1800]
columns.determinism: Deterministic within scenario (same event always gets same timestamp offset relative to phase)

columns.generation_rule: Random ephemeral port in range [1024, 65535]; one per event
columns.source_data: None (UNSW has no sport); random generator with scenario seed for reproducibility

GENERATION.rationale: UNSW lacks network context (hostnames, ports); these must be generated to create realistic IDS flows

INFERENCE.description: Derived from other columns using deterministic rules

derivations.inference_rule: Deterministic IP→hostname mapping: MD5(UNSW_srcIP + scenario_name) hashed to one of 11 internal hosts (User0-4, Enterprise0-2, Defender, OpHost0-2, OpServer0) based on modulo; OR external_XXXX if hash suggests external source
derivations.inputs: UNSW source IP (implicit, not in schema), scenario_name
derivations.rationale: Same UNSW IP always maps to same hostname within scenario for determinism

derivations.inference_rule: Deterministic IP→hostname mapping: MD5(UNSW_dstIP + scenario_name) hashed to hostname pool
Inferred from src_host prefix: User* → 'Subnet 1 (User)', Enterprise* → 'Subnet 2 (Enterprise)', OpHost* | OpServer* → 'Subnet 3 (Operational)', external* → 'External'
derivations.rationale: Hostnames encode subnet membership; mapping is deterministic

derivations.inference_rule: Inferred from dst_host prefix: same logic as src_subnet
Service-to-dport lookup: http→80, https→443, ftp→21, ssh→22, dns→53, smtp→25, smb→445, snmp→161, rdp→3389, telnet→23, etc. See port_service_mapping table below
derivations.rationale: IDS requires explicit port information; UNSW service names can be mapped to standard ports

INFERENCE.rationale: Derived deterministically from available UNSW data; no synthesis required

LABEL_TRANSFORMATION.description: Special case: UNSW binary label (0/1) transformed to categorical IDS label (Benign/Malicious/False Alarm)
LABEL_TRANSFORMATION.transformation_rule: Conversion happens in Steps 3-6: Step 3 generates malicious events (label='Malicious'), Step 4 generates benign events (label='Benign'), Step 5 generates false alarms (label='False Alarm'). Output label field is NOT a direct map from UNSW label; it's derived from synthesis strategy.
LABEL_TRANSFORMATION.rationale: UNSW distinguishes only Normal vs. Attack; IDS requires finer distinction (Benign vs. Malicious vs. False Alarm) based on scenario-specific synthesis decisions

DROPPED.description: Columns removed from IDS schema (not included in output)
reasons: Protocol-specific (window size, TCP sequence numbers, jitter, RTT)
Higher-order statistics (temporal context features, response body length)
Low generality (FTP-specific counters, HTTP method counts)
Temporal context that's hard to preserve (ct_srv_src, ct_dst_ltm)
DROPPED.rationale: IDS schema prioritizes network-level, protocol-agnostic features; application-layer and TCP-specific details add noise without improved generality

port_service_mapping.description: Standard port-to-service lookup used for dport inference in Pre-Step

column_accounting.total: 11 + 2 + 2 + 4 + 1 + 30 = 50 (45 UNSW + 5 net new accounting for aggregations)

key_insights: 11 of 21 IDS columns come directly from UNSW with no modification
2 aggregations handle directional data (sbytes/dbytes → bytes, spkts/dpkts → packets)
4 IDS columns are inferred from UNSW data using deterministic rules (subnet membership from hostname)
2 IDS columns (timestamp, sport) are synthetically generated; not derivable from UNSW
1 special transformation: UNSW binary label → IDS categorical label (determined in Steps 3-6, not Pre-Step)
30 UNSW columns dropped (primarily protocol-specific, high-order statistics, or temporal context)
